 {"id":"bd-1565j","title":"Diagram Diffing (Compare Two Diagram Versions)","description":"## Goal
Given two Mermaid sources (or IRs), compute and visually highlight differences: new nodes (green), removed nodes (red), changed edges (yellow), moved nodes (blue). Unique capability that browser-based Mermaid doesn't offer.

## Why
- Invaluable for PR reviews: "what changed in our architecture diagram?"
- The IR hashing (hash_ir) already computes structural fingerprints — diff can compare sub-hashes.
- Terminal-native diffing is instantaneous and integrates with git workflows.
- No existing tool provides visual graph diffs in terminal.

## Algorithm
1. Parse both sources into MermaidDiagramIr.
2. Match nodes by ID (string match) between old and new IR.
3. For matched nodes: compare label, shape, cluster membership, edges. Mark changed if different.
4. Unmatched in new IR: mark as "added" (green).
5. Unmatched in old IR: mark as "removed" (red, rendered as ghost nodes).
6. Edge diff: compare (from, to, label) tuples. Added edges green, removed red, changed yellow.
7. Layout uses the NEW IR as primary; removed nodes positioned near their old neighbors.

## Rendering
- Added nodes: green border + "+" marker
- Removed nodes: red border + dashed outline + "−" marker (ghost)
- Changed nodes: yellow border + "~" marker
- Unchanged nodes: normal rendering (dimmed slightly)
- Same color scheme for edges

## API
- diff_diagrams(old: &str, new: &str) → DiagramDiff
- render_diff(diff: &DiagramDiff, buf: &mut Buffer, area: Rect)
- Demo showcase: toggle diff mode, select "before" sample

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs (add diff_diagrams function)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (add render_diff method)

## Tests
- Unit: node addition/removal detection
- Unit: edge change detection
- Snapshot: before/after diagram with diff highlighting","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"DarkLynx","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:29:48.216517119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:35:23.478864014Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T01:35:23.478840881Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diffing","mermaid","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1565j","depends_on_id":"bd-1emsq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:17.625839545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":800,"issue_id":"bd-1565j","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"OrangeSpring claiming.  {"id":"bd-16k","title":"Unicode width corpus testing with WTF-8","description":"TESTING - UNICODE WIDTH CORPUS

## Background
Unicode width calculation is notoriously complex with edge cases around emoji, CJK, combining characters, and ZWJ sequences. A comprehensive test corpus is essential for correctness.

## Technical Requirements
- Test corpus categories:
  - Basic ASCII (width 1)
  - CJK Unified Ideographs (width 2)
  - Fullwidth ASCII variants (width 2)
  - Halfwidth Katakana (width 1)
  - Emoji with modifiers (width varies)
  - ZWJ sequences (flag emoji, family emoji)
  - Combining characters (width 0)
  - Control characters (width 0)
  - Ambiguous width characters (context-dependent)
- WTF-8 handling for unpaired surrogates
- Terminal-specific width (vs Unicode Standard)
- Test against unicode-width crate results

## Known Edge Cases
- 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 (family emoji ZWJ) - varies by terminal
- 🏳️‍🌈 (flag ZWJ) - often renders as 2 cells
- Regional indicators (flag pairs)
- Variation selectors (VS15/VS16)

## Design Rationale
Correctness in width calculation directly impacts rendering quality. The corpus captures real-world edge cases that simple tests miss.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Corpus with 1000+ test cases
- [ ] Categorized by width type
- [ ] Known terminal-specific behaviors documented
- [ ] Property tests for grapheme iteration
- [ ] Fuzz testing for malformed input
- [ ] Benchmark for width calculation performance

 {"id":"bd-16v2","title":"Action Timeline: Sketch Error Budgeting (Count-Min + PAC-Bayes)","description":"## Goal
Set Count-Min Sketch parameters with explicit error guarantees and document the bound in the UI/diagnostics.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Standard CMS bound: with width w and depth d, point-query error <= epsilon * N with probability >= 1 - delta where w = ceil(e/epsilon), d = ceil(ln(1/delta)).
Use a PAC-Bayes style calibration to tighten epsilon based on observed stream variance and validate with held-out samples.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Choose (w,d) from explicit (epsilon, delta) targets.
- Provide a runtime error estimate that is conservative and monotonic.
- Evidence ledger includes epsilon, delta, N, and observed calibration error.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_cms_bound_respected: synthetic stream respects epsilon with delta threshold.
- unit_parameter_selection: w,d computed correctly for given epsilon/delta.
- unit_pac_bayes_tightens: calibration reduces observed error vs raw bound.
- property_random_streams: observed error <= bound in >= (1-delta) trials.

## Perf
- Benchmark CMS update throughput and memory footprint; log results.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.1","title":"VFX perf harness: deterministic run + effect lock","description":"Background\n- Visual Effects perf profiling is noisy because AppModel initializes all screens and effects on startup. We need a deterministic, VFX-only harness to measure real frame cost without unrelated initialization overhead.\n\nGoals\n- Provide a repeatable harness that locks to a single VFX effect, fixed terminal size, fixed tick cadence, and fixed duration/frames.\n- Ensure the harness avoids initializing non‑VFX screens entirely (fast-path or VFX-only AppModel path).\n\nScope / Work Items\n- Add or extend CLI/env options (FTUI_DEMO_* family) to force: screen=VisualEffects, effect=<name>, fixed cols/rows, fixed tick cadence (Hz or ms), and auto-exit after N frames or N ms.\n- Implement a VFX-only startup path that bypasses heavy ScreenStates::default and constructs only VisualEffects state (plus minimal runtime plumbing).\n- Provide a single documented command line for deterministic profiling and for CI/e2e usage.\n- Emit structured JSONL logs for harness runs: init metadata, effect name, size, frame index, hash, timing, and seed/time control.\n\nConstraints\n- No visual quality/resolution reduction and no algorithmic shortcuts that alter output.\n- Deterministic output for identical inputs (seed/time, tick cadence, size).\n- Prefer existing CLI/config surfaces; avoid new files unless absolutely necessary.\n\nTesting + Logging\n- Add/extend an e2e test that runs the harness twice and asserts identical hash sequences.\n- JSONL logging must include: run_id, effect, size, tick_ms, frame_idx, hash, seed/time.\n- Provide clear failure diagnostics (first mismatched frame, expected vs actual hash).\n\nNotes\n- This harness is a prerequisite for meaningful profiling and later optimization.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Single-line deterministic harness command exists and is documented in code comments/tests.\n2) Harness avoids initializing non‑VFX screens (verified via logs or a test).\n3) Two runs with identical inputs produce identical per‑frame hashes.\n4) JSONL logs include all required fields and are schema‑valid.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:56:53.029445192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:10:48.205149356Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:10:48.205130971Z","close_reason":"Completed (VFX harness + deterministic hashes + JSONL logging)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:56:53.029445192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.10","title":"VFX perf regression guard + CI evidence logs","description":"Background
- We need a regression guard so VFX perf gains remain stable and detectable in CI.
- The guard should be deterministic, hash-verified, and report clear diagnostics.

Goals
- Add a VFX perf regression gate using the harness + timing logs.
- Fail fast on significant regressions with actionable JSONL diagnostics.

Scope / Work Items
- Define baseline thresholds per effect/size from bd-3e1t.5.7 (p50/p95/p99 total_ms).
- Add an automated check (test or E2E script) that runs the harness at fixed seed/time and compares metrics against thresholds.
- Validate JSONL schema and include first-mismatch diagnostics (effect, size, frame_idx, expected vs actual hash, timing deltas).
- Ensure the guard is deterministic and does not introduce runtime overhead when disabled.

Constraints
- No visual quality or resolution changes.
- Use existing harness/logging; avoid new files unless required by test infra.

Tests / Validation
- Guard fails on synthetic regression and passes on baseline.
- JSONL logs are schema-stable and include perf + hash fields.

Dependencies
- Requires timing instrumentation (bd-3e1t.5.2), golden hashes (bd-3e1t.5.5), and baseline pack (bd-3e1t.5.7).","acceptance_criteria":"1) Automated guard compares p50/p95/p99 against baseline thresholds for defined effects and sizes.\n2) Guard fails with actionable diagnostics (effect, size, frame_idx, expected vs actual hash, timing deltas).\n3) JSONL schema validation passes and hashes remain stable for baseline runs.","notes":"Added PTY perf regression guard in crates/ftui-demo-showcase/tests/visual_effects_pty.rs. New test pty_vfx_perf_regression_guard runs 5 effects x 2 sizes (80x24, 120x40) at 120 frames, tick_ms=16, seed=0, parses vfx_perf_summary, and enforces p50/p95/p99 budgets from bd-3e1t.5.7 with 2x CI multiplier.
